title: Dev server runs with debug=True bound to 0.0.0.0
evidence: app.py:143 app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0', port=5000) — Werkzeug interactive debugger allows arbitrary code execution and is reachable on all interfaces
suggestion: Disable debug in any non-local context and bind to 127.0.0.1, or gate via an env var

title: year is nullable=False but not validated → 500 on POST without year
evidence: app.py:26 year=db.Column(..., nullable=False) vs app.py:73 year=data.get('year') — omitting year yields None and the commit fails with a generic 500 (app.py:81-83) instead of a 400
suggestion: Either make year nullable or validate its presence and return 400

title: Tests operate on the app's real books.db, not an isolated DB
evidence: test_app.py:7 imports the configured app; setUp (test_app.py:15) create_all and tearDown (test_app.py:31) db.drop_all() run against the SQLite file bound in app.py:15, so running tests mutates/destroys the dev database
suggestion: Override SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI to sqlite:///:memory: in setUp for isolation

title: No pinned dependency manifest (requirements.txt / pyproject.toml)
evidence: run root has no requirements.txt or pyproject.toml; README.md:31 only instructs 'pip install flask flask-sqlalchemy' with no versions
suggestion: Add a requirements.txt pinning flask and flask-sqlalchemy for reproducible setup

title: Duplicate isbn returns generic 500 instead of a 409/400
evidence: app.py:27 isbn unique=True; a POST with a duplicate isbn raises IntegrityError caught at app.py:81-83 and returned as 500 'Failed to create book'
suggestion: Detect uniqueness violations and return 409 Conflict with a clear message

title: Very low token efficiency for a small task
evidence: scores.json token_efficiency=0.0056; _agent_stdout.log is ~49MB, indicating a large amount of agent output for a single-file Flask CRUD app
suggestion: None required for correctness; noted for cross-run efficiency comparison
